title: Israel launches fresh strikes on Lebanon despite Trump criticism
snippet: Speaking on Tuesday, Trump said Israel's PM Benjamin Netanyahu needed "to be more responsible with respect to Lebanon".

title: Status quo at Jerusalem's holiest site under threat as Israeli nationalists flout rules
snippet: Israeli nationalists are increasingly flouting a convention on how faiths share the al-Aqsa mosque compound.

title: 'They came with machetes' - deadline looms for migrants to leave South Africa
snippet: Protesters have set 30 June as the date for all undocumented migrants to leave the country.

title: Search for six-year-old Ebola patient after armed men storm DR Congo hospital
snippet: Health facilities have come under attack during the current outbreak as a result of misinformation and fear.

title: Group planned to attack White House UFC event using snipers and drones, FBI says
snippet: In newly unsealed court filings, the government says the group allegedly expressed grievances about corruption, the Epstein files, and data centres.

title: Norway's crown princess undergoes successful lung transplant, palace says
snippet: Crown Princess Mette-Marit, who suffered from pulmonary fibrosis, will now spend several weeks recovering in hospital.

title: Iran sends tankers loaded with oil past US military blockade
snippet: Three Iranian tankers loaded with crude oil have passed the US blockade line in the Gulf of Oman, ship-tracking data shows.

title: Nigerian man jailed for storing human faeces outside his home
snippet: Neighbours said the stench from the property had become so overwhelming that it was impossible to relax at home.

title: Equatorial Guinea government resigns after failing to meet targets
snippet: The vice-president says the government had barely reached 10% of its targets, without specifying them.

title: Leader of South Africa's second biggest party wants his predecessor sacked as minister
snippet: John Steenhuisen is one of South Africa's best-known politicians after leading the DA for seven years.

title: Snap unveils £1,995 smart glasses after previous flops
snippet: The augmented reality glasses from Snapchat's parent company are expected to ship in autumn.

title: Five big questions about the UK's under-16s social media ban
snippet: A ban is coming - but it's still not clear what it will mean for sites including Roblox, YouTube and WhatsApp.

title: When will social media ban start, and which apps will be affected?
snippet: The measures will see apps including TikTok and Snapchat banned for UK teens early in 2027.

title: What is Helium-3 and could we get it from the moon?
snippet: Helium-3 is expensive and demand is forecast to soar, so some are planning to mine it on the moon.

title: Social media ban - bold and blunt, but no silver bullet
snippet: The BBC's technology editor Zoe Kleinman on the big changes coming down the line for young people online.
